body.Headquarters: San Francisco, CA, USA (legally incorporated; also registered in Singapore)
body.Industry: Open-Source Backend-as-a-Service (BaaS) / Database Technology / Cloud Infrastructure / Developer Tools
body.LatestValuation: $10.5 billion post-money (as of June 4, 2026, Series F)
body.FundingRounds: Seed ($6M, October 2020, led by Y Combinator & Coatue) | Series A (2021, undisclosed) | Series B (April 2022, led by Felicis Ventures & Lightspeed) | Series C ($80M, September 2024, led by Peak XV Partners & Craft Ventures; PitchBook estimated ~$765M post-money valuation) | Series D ($200M, April 2025, led by Accel; $2B valuation) | Series E ($100M, October 3, 2025, led by Accel & Peak XV; $5B pre-money valuation) | Series F ($500M, June 4, 2026, led by GIC; $10.5B post-money valuation)
body.KeyInvestors: Institutional: GIC (Series F lead), Accel (Series D/E/F), Peak XV Partners (Series C/D/E/F), Y Combinator (Seed), Coatue (Seed/E/F), Felicis Ventures (Series B+), Craft Ventures (Series C+), Lightspeed (Series B), Square Peg Ventures (Series E), Figma Ventures (Series E), Stripe (Series F), Salesforce Ventures (Series F), Mozilla Ventures | Angel Investors: Tom Preston-Werner, Nat Friedman, Guillermo Rauch, James Tamplin, Yuri Sagalov
body.EmployeeCountEstimate: ~350–359 employees as of mid-2026 (per Tracxn: 359 as of May 31, 2026; CNBC June 2026: 350 employees); ~124 employees reported in early 2025
body.RevenueEstimate: Sacra estimates ~$170M ARR as of May 2026, up from ~$101M ARR at end of 2025; CBInsights estimates 2024 revenue at ~$20M; other sources estimate 2024 revenue at ~$16M with 2025 projections of ~$27M (these lower figures may reflect recognized revenue vs. ARR)
body.GrowthMetrics: 4M+ developers on platform (as of Oct 2025); 250,000+ customers as of June 2026; database launches grew 600% YoY leading up to June 2026; over 60% of new databases launched by AI tools; user base more than doubled between Series E (Oct 2025) and Series F (June 2026); valuation grew 500%+ in ~1 year (from ~$765M Series C estimate to $5B Series E); valuation doubled again in ~8 months to $10.5B at Series F; 100% distributed team across 37 countries; enterprise customers include PwC, McDonald's, and GitHub Next
body.RecentDealsAndExits: Acquisitions: Logflare (2021, analytics/log management); OrioleDB / Oriole (acquired April 15, 2024, advanced Postgres storage engine). No IPO or exit announced; company has stated no near-term IPO plans. Available on AWS Marketplace since December 2025.
body.ComparableCompanies: Firebase (Google) — primary direct competitor and open-source alternative positioning; Neon (serverless Postgres); YugabyteDB (distributed SQL); PubNub (real-time data); Couchbase; PlanetScale; Railway; Render; Appwrite
body.DataConfidenceNotes: Valuation and funding data through Series F (June 2026) is well-sourced from CNBC, PRNewswire, TechCrunch, and Tracxn. Revenue figures vary significantly by source: Sacra's $170M ARR (May 2026) vs. earlier estimates of $16–20M for 2024 recognized revenue — this discrepancy likely reflects ARR vs. recognized revenue methodology differences and rapid 2025–2026 growth. Employee count of 359 (Tracxn) and 350 (CNBC) are consistent and reliable for mid-2026. Series F valuation of $10.5B is confirmed by multiple independent sources. Total funding of $1.04B is confirmed. Earlier round sizes (Seed, Series A, Series B) may have minor data gaps as exact amounts were not always publicly disclosed.
